operations.name: agm-analyze
operations.description: Analyze scope, hidden problems, impacts, and trade-offs
conditionalInputs: target_kind when repository evidence cannot resolve the affected discipline
operations.example: $agm-analyze requested_by=Billy target_files=src/orders.ts objective="Explain duplicate requests; do not edit"
instructions: Inspect the full target and affected callers before conclusions.
Return facts, inferences, unknowns, impacts, viable options, and a recommendation; never edit product artifacts.

operations.name: agm-diagnose
operations.description: Diagnose a problem to a proven root cause
conditionalInputs: reproduction evidence when the symptom is not observable from the repository
instructions: Reproduce or trace the symptom, separate causes from correlations, and test bounded hypotheses.
Return the proven root cause, evidence, affected path, and proposed fix; do not apply the fix.

operations.name: agm-simulate
operations.description: Simulate scenarios, risks, and observable outcomes
conditionalInputs: inputs and observables when they cannot be derived from the scenario
instructions: Model the initial state, transitions, branches, failure modes, and observables.
Label assumptions and confidence; do not present a simulation as measured runtime fact.

operations.name: agm-plan
conditionalInputs: decision-owner choice when multiple material approaches remain viable
instructions: Reverse-engineer current behavior, dependencies, callers, tests, and integration order before planning.
Each step names target, action, reason, verification, and decision-owner gate; do not implement.

operations.name: agm-design
operations.description: Design a user flow, behavior, and acceptance criteria
conditionalInputs: target_kind when FE/BE placement remains ambiguous
phase for FE/BE work when repository evidence cannot resolve it
instructions: Define actors, entry points, states, transitions, validation, failure/recovery, accessibility, and measurable acceptance.
Use the target discipline selected by the conditional routing below; do not implement.

operations.name: agm-architect
operations.description: Design boundaries, contracts, and migration trade-offs
conditionalInputs: decision-owner approval before an architecture record becomes approved
operations.example: $agm-architect requested_by=Billy objective="Choose ownership boundary for order status"
instructions: Map ownership, contracts, data flow, runtime/deployment effects, alternatives, migration, rollback, and reversible decisions.
Produce a proposed decision record; only recorded authority may approve it.

operations.name: agm-review
operations.description: Review code or artifacts with evidence-backed findings
conditionalInputs: review_scope when the requester wants narrower coverage than the default
instructions: Review correctness, regressions, contracts/data, maintainability, relevant performance, and tests in that order.
Report actionable findings by severity with file/line, evidence, and impact; do not fix them.

conditionalReferences.when: the target is FE, BE, or SQL
conditionalReferences.why: route to the current target pattern only

operations.name: agm-history
operations.description: Query workflow attribution and audit-storage status by person, task, event, or UTC time range
requiredInputs: at least one useful person, task, event, or time-range query
conditionalInputs: explicit UTC offset for timestamp inputs
instructions: Run the workspace history command and answer from versioned workflow evidence, not conversational recall.
Separate requester, execution identity, claimed files, Git authorship, and storage durability.

operations.name: agm-refactor-fe
operations.description: Refactor frontend code using an explicit behavior mode
operations.deliverable: bounded frontend changes plus verification and Result Package
conditionalInputs: target_kind and phase when repository evidence cannot resolve them
decision-owner approval for logic change
instructions: Prove current behavior and select exactly one refactor mode before editing.
Preserve or change behavior only as that mode allows; run frontend pattern gates and proportional verification.

operations.name: agm-refactor-be
operations.description: Refactor backend code using an explicit behavior mode
operations.deliverable: bounded backend changes plus verification and Result Package
conditionalInputs: backend_profile for be-main
decision-owner approval for logic or contract change
instructions: Prove current behavior and select exactly one refactor mode before editing.
Preserve architecture, routes, responses, messages, data, and public contracts unless authority explicitly approves change.

operations.name: agm-refactor-sql
operations.description: Refactor SQL using an explicit behavior mode
operations.deliverable: bounded SQL changes plus static/contract verification and Result Package
conditionalInputs: decision-owner approval for data, result-shape, transaction, or error-contract change
instructions: Load matching schema references as facts before editing and select exactly one refactor mode.
Preserve result sets, transactions, side effects, error mapping, and deployment idempotency unless authorized otherwise.

references.why: current SQL contract and message gate

operations.name: agm-qa
operations.description: Verify tracked work in a separate product-read-only context
conditionalInputs: qa_mode=full when a schema full-QA trigger applies
instructions: Apply qa-and-done.md as the complete policy; reopen integrated artifacts, map requirements to evidence, and rerun selected claims.
Return only the canonical status and evidence; do not copy policy text into the report.

references.why: single QA, correction, and completion policy

conditionalReferences.when: the task is FE, BE, or SQL
conditionalReferences.why: select exactly the matching pattern and Detect gates

operations.name: agm-create-unit-test
operations.description: Create target-specific unit or regression tests
operations.deliverable: selected tests plus verification and Result Package
conditionalInputs: requester selection when behaviors were not already specified
backend_profile for be-main
instructions: Inspect the existing test framework and propose a numbered risk/behavior list when coverage was not specified.
Create only selected tests and follow the matching target discipline and naming.

operations.name: agm-create-feature
operations.description: Create a target-specific FE, BE, batch, library, or SQL feature
operations.deliverable: confirmed vertical slice plus verification and Result Package
conditionalInputs: target_kind when placement evidence is absent
backend_profile for be-main
full command approval for a new project scaffold
operations.example: $agm-create-feature requested_by=Billy target_kind=be-main backend_profile=agmws objective="Add cancel-order endpoint"
instructions: Inspect existing structure and propose the smallest complete slice with every output path before writing.
Use the matching target discipline; new project scaffolds require the exact command and working-directory approval.

operations.name: agm-create-prompt
operations.description: Create provider-specific Leader, executor, and QA prompts
conditionalInputs: target_kind/backend_profile for target-specific work
decision-owner approval before execution
instructions: Run staged elicitation, build one execution SoT per task, and create separate Leader/executor/QA prompts when implementation is delegated.
Generation ends in draft; never execute the generated prompt in this operation.

operations.name: agm-exec
operations.description: Execute one decision-owner-approved generated prompt as the execution source of truth with checkpoints, deviation stops, and a Result Package
operations.deliverable: integrated Result Package awaiting canonical QA
requiredInputs: approved prompt path or resumable task_id
conditionalInputs: decision-owner choice when a material deviation exceeds the approved prompt
instructions: Require prompt_status=owner-approved and execute its ordered steps as the task source of truth.
Stop and report structured deviation evidence when a material change exceeds scope; return the Result Package and integration artifact.

references.why: native activity, workspace, and integration contract
